name: Dunne Law Offices, P.C.
description_short: Philadelphia-based bankruptcy attorney offering Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filing services, plus credit repair and debt collector defense. First consultation is free.
description_long: Dunne Law Offices, P.C. is a bankruptcy law practice operating in Philadelphia and accessible via their website at thephiladelphiabankruptcyattorney.com. The firm is led by Stephen Dunne, who specializes in personal bankruptcy representation and related financial legal matters. The practice has built its brand around offering accessible entry into bankruptcy services with no cost for initial consultations, lowering barriers for consumers in financial distress. The firm operates with a clear client-focused philosophy, marketing their services under the framework of 'Restart, Rebuild, Recover.' Dunne Law Offices offers comprehensive bankruptcy filing services covering both Chapter 7 (liquidation/debt elimination) and Chapter 13 (reorganization/repayment plan) bankruptcy proceedings. Beyond bankruptcy filing, they provide credit repair services focused on correcting errors in credit reports, debt collection harassment defense under federal law, and general financial counseling. The firm distinguishes itself through free initial consultations, direct attorney availability (Stephen Dunne appears to handle consultations personally based on website language), and educational content addressing common bankruptcy questions like post-bankruptcy home buying timelines, wage garnishment relief, and employer notification concerns. Their blog demonstrates engagement with timely topics like COVID-19 relief, SBA loans, and EIDL programs relevant to bankruptcy-adjacent financial recovery. The honest assessment is that while the website demonstrates solid legal expertise and consumer education, the firm is a single-attorney or small practice with no visible information about credentials, experience years, disciplinary history, client reviews, or success rates. The website claims one client eliminated $8.8 million in debt in 103 days, which strains credibility for a typical bankruptcy practice and may represent an exceptional outlier or require verification of underlying circumstances.
pros: Free initial consultation removes cost barrier for financially struggling consumers
Offers both Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 services, allowing attorney to guide clients to appropriate bankruptcy chapter
Addresses debt collector harassment and knows federal compliance rules (Fair Debt Collection Practices Act)
Provides credit report error correction services in addition to bankruptcy filing
Educational website content covers practical post-bankruptcy questions (home buying, employer notification, wage garnishment)
Direct phone contact and chat booking available (215-551-7109)
Addresses multiple financial problems comprehensively (bankruptcy, credit repair, collector defense)
cons: No visible client testimonials, reviews, or case results beyond one questionable claim ($8.8M debt eliminated in 103 days)
No information about attorney credentials, years of experience, bankruptcy certifications, or bar standing
Appears to be solo or very small practice with no team transparency or backup attorney availability
Website lacks pricing information, fee structures, or clear explanation of what free consultation covers vs. paid services
No information about whether they handle business bankruptcy (Chapter 11) or student loan discharge
best_for: Philadelphia-area consumers facing overwhelming credit card or medical debt seeking Chapter 7 debt elimination
Employed individuals with regular income who need debt restructuring through Chapter 13 repayment plans
Consumers being actively harassed by debt collectors seeking legal representation and FDCPA violations defense
People with credit report errors who need professional dispute assistance alongside bankruptcy considerations
diagnosis: Dunne Law Offices is best for Philadelphia-area consumers in Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy situations who want direct attorney access without upfront cost and need debt collector defense. The primary caveat is the complete absence of verifiable credentials, client reviews, experience documentation, or realistic case outcome data—consumers should independently verify the attorney's standing and seek second opinions before committing to representation.
services: Credit report error correction and dispute
Debt collection harassment defense and FDCPA violation claims
FHA mortgage guideline counsel for post-bankruptcy home purchase
SBA and EIDL loan navigation for bankruptcy clients

places_enrichment.formatted_address: 1515 Market St suite 1200-b, Philadelphia, PA 19102, USA
